label: Support vector & kernel machines

node.label: Support vector & kernel machines
node.definition: Support-vector machines maximize a separating margin; kernel variants obtain nonlinear boundaries through similarity functions that act as implicit feature maps.
node.why_it_matters: The role of Support vector & kernel machines is to connect Kernels & implicit feature maps, Margin, ranking & metric objectives to downstream capabilities including Tabular predictive analytics & decision support.
examples: substrate for Tabular predictive analytics & decision support
combined with Kernels & implicit feature maps
limitations: Its inductive bias, data demand, optimization behavior, and compute profile can make it unsuitable outside the regimes where it was validated. Its direct dependencies include Kernels & implicit feature maps, Margin, ranking & metric objectives.

dependencies.rationale: Support vector & kernel machines is assembled in part from the mechanism or model component Kernels & implicit feature maps.
Support vector & kernel machines is learned, selected, or evaluated using the objective family Margin, ranking & metric objectives.
Support vector & kernel machines follows the learning setup expressed by Supervised learning.
